coredata.dc:title: Selective detection of Fe3+ by Ag2S QDs and construction of the IMPLICATION gate
coredata.prism:copyright: © 2024 The Authors. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Liu, Lijun
Ga, Lu
Ai, Jun
coredata.dc:description: Several studies have demonstrated that Ag2S QDs are promising near-infrared II-emitting nanoprobes. They Ag2S QDs can be used in the fields of bio-imaging, fluorescence detection and photodetectors. In this work, we achieved the first successful construction of a dual-input logic gate IMPLICATION gate based on the specific detection of Fe3+ by silver sulfide quantum dots and the reduction of Fe3+ by ascorbic acid. The Ag2S QDs were successfully prepared from glutathione and silver nitrate and characterized by fluorescence spectroscopy analysis, XPS analysis, and transmission electron microscopy TEM analysis. The prepared Ag2S QDs can achieve selective detection of Fe3+ with a detection limit of LOD of 0.15 mM. This work provides a new method for the detection of Fe3+.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Ag2S QDs
Fe3+
Fluorescence sensor
IMPLICATION gate
Selective detection

Fluorescence spectroscopy, TEM and UV absorption spectroscopy characterization of Ag2S QDs
Selective detection analysis of Ag2S QDs

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (a) Fluorescence excitation emission map of Ag2S QDs. (b) TEM image of Ag2S QDs. (c) Histogram of the particle size distribution of Ag2S QDs. (d) UV absorption spectra of Ag2S QDs after dilution at different multiples.
(a) XPS full range measurement spectra of Ag2S QDs. (b) Ag 3d, (c) C 1 s, (d) S 2p, (e)N 1 s, (f) Atomic percentage of elements from XPS studies.
(a) Photobleaching fluorescence spectra of Ag2S QDs. (b) Scatter plot of the photobleaching properties of Ag2S QDs. (c) Same emission multi-excitation fluorescence spectrogram.

(a) Fluorescence spectra of selective analysis of small molecules by Ag2S QDs. (b) Histogram of selective analysis of Ag2S QDs for small molecules. (c) Fluorescence spectra of selective analysis of Ag2S QDs for metal ions. (d) Histogram of selective analysis of Ag2S QDs for metal ions. (e) Plot of fluorescence change after the introduction of 400 μM-10 mM of Fe3+ in Ag2S QDs solution. (f) Plot of fluorescence intensity of Ag2S QDs solution versus Fe3+ concentration.

(a) Fluorescence spectra of the IMPLICATION gate with Fe3+ and AA as input and Ag2S QDs fluorescence intensity as output. (b) Histogram of the IMPLICATION gate with Fe3+ and AA as input and Ag2S QDs fluorescence intensity as output.
The process of preparing Ag2S QDs and the schematic diagram of Fe3+ detection.
The spiked recoveries of Fe3+ in actual water samples.

head.ce:title: Selective detection of Fe3+ by Ag2S QDs and construction of the IMPLICATION gate

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Chemistry and Enviromental Science, Inner Mongolia Key Laboratory of Environmental Chemistry, Inner Mongolia Normal University, 81 Zhaowudalu, Hohhot 010022, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Chemistry and Enviromental Science
Inner Mongolia Key Laboratory of Environmental Chemistry

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Chemistry and Enviromental Science, Inner Mongolia Key Laboratory of Environmental Chemistry, Inner Mongolia Normal University, 81 zhaowudalu, Hohhot 010022, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Pharmacy, Inner Mongolia Medical University, Jinchuankaifaqu, Hohhot 010110, China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Pharmacy, Inner Mongolia Medical University, Jinchuankaifaqu, Hohhot,010110, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Several studies have demonstrated that Ag2S QDs are promising near-infrared II-emitting nanoprobes. They Ag2S QDs can be used in the fields of bio-imaging, fluorescence detection and photodetectors. In this work, we achieved the first successful construction of a dual-input logic gate IMPLICATION gate based on the specific detection of Fe3+ by silver sulfide quantum dots and the reduction of Fe3+ by ascorbic acid. The Ag2S QDs were successfully prepared from glutathione and silver nitrate and characterized by fluorescence spectroscopy analysis, XPS analysis, and transmission electron microscopy TEM analysis. The prepared Ag2S QDs can achieve selective detection of Fe3+ with a detection limit of LOD of 0.15 mM. This work provides a new method for the detection of Fe3+.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: No data was used for the research described in the article.

ce:section.ce:para: In this chapter work, Ag2S QDs with GSH as S source were prepared by one-step microwave method. The synthesized Ag2S QDs were analyzed by fluorescence spectroscopy, XPS, TEM and UV absorbance.Ag2S QDs can be used as fluorescent probes for Fe3+ detection and combined with the reduction of Fe3+ by AA, the IMPLICATION gate can be completed construction.

All reagents in this work were used directly without further purification treatment if not specified, and the water used during the experiments was ultrapure water (18.2 MQ∙cm) prepared by the Milli.Q. All drugs are analytical purity: AgNO3, C10H17N3O6S, FeCl3∙6H2O, AA(C6H8O6). Instruments used include: Fluorescence spectroscopy (F97pro), Transmission microscope (JEOL-2100F), UV–Visible Spectroscopy (U-2900), Centrifuge (D1008), Microwave Oven (P70D21N1P-S1).

The synthesis procedure was modified based on the published article [33]. The specific experimental procedure was as follows: 1.2293 g of glutathione (GSH) was weighed and dissolved in 40 mL of ultrapure water, and then silver nitrate (AgNO3) solution (10 mL, 0.4 M) was added, at which time the solution changed to a white flocculent. The pH of the solution was adjusted immediately by slowly adding 1 M sodium hydroxide (NaOH) solution to the beaker until the solution became a clarified yellow solution, at which time the pH of the solution was about 10. The solution was stirred at room temperature for 20 min and then heated in a microwave oven over medium heat for 4–5 min, at which time the solution became a clarified red-brown color, indicating the successful preparation of silver sulfide quantum dots. The synthesized Ag2S QDs solution was washed with ethanol (v/v = 1/1) and the supernatant was removed by centrifugation at 15000 rpm for 10 min before proceeding to subsequent experiments.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analysis of specific detection of Fe3+ by Ag2S QDs
ce:section.ce:para: Five biological small molecules: Glycine (Gly), Glucose (Glu), Ascorbic Acid (AA), D-Penicillamine (DPA), Thiopropene (MPG) and 10 metal ions (Fe3+, Fe2+, Pb2+, Cu2+, Hg2+, Co2+, Ni2+, Al3+, Mn2+, Ca2+) were selected for the specific detection analysis. The effects of these 15 analyzed substances on the fluorescence intensity of Ag2S QDs were investigated. At room temperature, 400 μL of the prepared Ag2S QDs and 100 μL of the biological small molecules or metal ions to be detected (both at 0.1 M concentration) were placed in 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes, incubated for 45 min at 15000 rpm and centrifuged for 10 min at an excitation wavelength of 359 nm, and the fluorescence intensity of these mixed solutions at an emission wavelength of 437 nm was measured by a fluorescence spectrophotometer. The fluorescence intensities of these mixed solutions were measured by fluorescence spectrophotometer at the excitation wavelength of 359 nm.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Linear range of specific detection of Fe3+
ce:section.ce:para: At room temperature, 100 μL Fe3+ in the concentration range of 1 mM −10 mM were mixed with 300 μL Ag2S QDs in 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes, respectively. After co-incubation for 45 min in a light-protected place, the fluorescence intensity of the mixed solutions was measured at 359 nm excitation wavelength and 437 nm emission wavelength by centrifugation at 15000 rpm for 10 min. All reactions were performed in ultra-pure water (18.2 MQ∙cm) environment prepared by Milli.Q ultra-pure water system.

Take four separate 400 μL portions of Ag2S QDs into 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes. After introducing different combinations of inputs into the Ag2S QDs, they were incubated at room temperature and protected from light. The operation of the IMPLICATION gate was achieved with the change in fluorescence intensity of the Ag2S QDs as the output. In this case, the input or not of Fe3+ and ascorbic acid (AA) was noted as input “1” and “0”, respectively, and the fluorescence signal was noted as whether the fluorescence signal was quench or not as “0” and “1” respectively. For the input combination, after adding 400 μL of Ag2S QDs and 100 μL of analyte (both at a concentration of 0.1 M), a certain volume of ultrapure water was added to keep the total volume of the solution constant (600 μL). When the input combination is (0,0), 200 μL of ultrapure water is added to 400 μL of Ag2S QDs and incubated for 45 min with protection from light; when the input combination is (1,0), 100 μL of Fe3+ and 100 μL of ultrapure water are added to 400 μL of Ag2S QDs; when the input combination is (0,1), 100 μL of ultrapure water is added to 400 μL of Ag2S QDs and 100 μL of ultrapure water are added to 400 μL of Ag2S QDs. In all of the above combinations, input 1 was introduced into the Ag2S QDs for 30 min to make the reaction complete, and then input 2 was introduced and incubated for 45 min, followed by centrifugation at 15000 rpm for 10 min to measure the fluorescence intensity. Fluorescence intensity was measured.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescence spectroscopy, TEM and UV absorption spectroscopy characterization of Ag2S QDs
ce:section.ce:para: Among many semiconductor quantum dots, silver sulfide quantum dots do not contain toxic heavy metals and have good eco-friendly benefits, and can be used as fluorescent nanoprobes for selective detection of metal ions. In this experiment, highly fluorescent water-soluble silver sulfide quantum dots (Ag2S QDs) were rapidly synthesized in a short time (4–5 min) by a simple one-step microwave method with silver nitrate (AgNO3) as the silver source and glutathione (GSH) as the sulfur source, and Ag2S QDs were characterized by fluorescence spectrophotometer at excitation and emission wavelengths, as shown in Fig. 1(a), at 359 nm excitation, the Ag2S QDs showed high fluorescence emission intensity at 437 nm, and after washing the red-brown silver sulfide quantum dot solution with ethanol, the prepared Ag2S QDs emitted purplish-red fluorescence under 365 nm UV lamp irradiation. Transmission electron microscopy was used to probe the morphology of GSH-Ag2S QDs, as shown in Fig. 1(b). Based on the transmission electron micrograph (TEM), it can be seen that the GSH-Ag2S QDs are well dispersed and spherical in shape, with quantum dot diameters ranging from about 4 nm to 9 nm (Fig. 1(c)) and an average particle size of about 5.6 nm, and no obvious large particle deposits can be observed in the field of view. As shown in Fig. 1(d), the intensity of the absorption peaks of Ag2S QDs decreased gradually with the increase of the dilution, and the position of the absorption peaks also gradually shifted blue as compared with the UV absorption peaks of undiluted Ag2S QDs. (See Scheme 1.)

Five main peaks can be observed in the full spectrum (Fig. 2(a)), located at 367.9 eV, 285.2 eV, 162.0 eV, 399.8 eV and 531.4 eV, corresponding to Ag 3d, C 1 s, S 2p, N 1 s and O 1 s, respectively. It indicates that the successfully prepared Ag2S QDs are mainly composed of five elements, Ag, C, S, N and O. As shown in Fig. 2(b), there are two typical peaks at 367.6 eV and 373.6 eV in the high-resolution Ag 3d spectrum, corresponding to Ag 3d5/2 and Ag 3d3/2, respectively. as shown in Fig. 2(c), there are three peaks at 284.8 eV, 285.9 eV and 287.9 eV in the high-resolution spectrum of C 1 s, which can correspond to CC, CO and CO, indicating the presence of carboxyl functional groups in the synthesized silver sulfide quantum dots. Fig. 2(d) shows the presence of S 2p binding energy at 161.7 eV and 163.0 eV, corresponding to CS bonds of S 2p3/2 and S 2p1/2, respectively. As shown in Fig. 2(e), two peaks exist in the XPS spectra of N 1 s at 399.7 eV and 401.5 eV, corresponding to the CN, NH2 binding energies, respectively. Therefore, the presence of carboxyl and amino functional groups on the surface of Ag2S QDs demonstrates that the prepared Ag2S QDs are characterized by good water dispersion and high stability. The atomic percentage of each element is shown in the Fig. 2(f).

To investigate the anti-photobleaching performance of the prepared GSH-Ag2S QDs at room temperature, the fluorescence value changes of the Ag2S QDs were measured every 5 min by continuous irradiation with a 365 nm UV lamp for 1 h. The results are demonstrated in Fig. 3(a)(b), where the fluorescence intensity of Ag2S QDs exhibited a slight decrease within each 5-min time gradient, and the fluorescence intensity of the quantum dots decreased by about 700 over an overall period of 60 min. The variation of the emission intensity of Ag2S QDs under different excitations was also characterized as shown in Fig. 3(c). It can be seen from the figure that the position of the fluorescence emission peak of Ag2S QDs is basically constant in the wavelength range from 280 nm to 380 nm, and the fluorescence intensity reaches a maximum at 360 nm, which corresponds to the previous fluorescence spectrogram.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Selective detection analysis of Ag2S QDs
ce:section.ce:para: Fe3+ is a common heavy metal ion, and the detection of heavy metal ions plays an important role in human health and environmental protection. In the experiments of this chapter, as shown in Fig. 4 , five small molecules including glycine (Gly), glucose (Glu), ascorbic acid (AA), D-penicillamine (DPA), thiopropene (MPG) and 10 small molecules including Fe3+, Fe2+, Pb2+, Cu2+, Hg2+, Co2+, Ni2+, Al3+, Mn2+, Ca2+ were selected Ten metal ions were co-incubated with Ag2S QDs for selective analysis. For the small molecules, it is easy to see that several typical small molecules selected have little effect on the fluorescence intensity of Ag2S QDs. For metal ions, among the selected several metal ions, only Fe3+ strongly affected the fluorescence of Ag2S QDs, and the fluorescence of Ag2S QDs was close to complete quench after the introduction of Fe3+. Therefore, it can be demonstrated that Ag2S QDs exhibit specific detection of Fe3+ in the presence of several interferents and are a new method for the detection of Fe3+ with high selectivity.

The fluorescence emission intensity was characterized for the reaction of different concentrations of Fe3+ with Ag2S QDs solution. As shown in Fig. 4(e), the fluorescence intensity of Ag2S QDs showed a gradual decrease with the increase of Fe3+ concentration in the range of 400 μM-10 mM. This phenomenon can be observed in Fig. 4(f), where the fluorescence intensity of Ag2S QDs showed a linear negative correlation with the concentration of the Fe3+ with R2 = 0.9416, and the detection limit LOD was 0.15 mM with standard deviation floating in the range of 1.15% ∼ 2.65%.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Detection of Co2+ in actual water samples
ce:section.ce:para: To further evaluate the feasibility of this method for the detection of Fe3+. GSH-Ag2S QDs were used for the detection of Fe3+ in laboratory tap water. The spiked recoveries of Fe3+ in laboratory tap water are shown in Table 1 , and the recoveries were about 91.1% ∼ 113.3%, indicating that the method can be applied to the detection of Fe3+ in actual water samples.

The IMPLICATION gate is the most widely used of the two-input logic gates. Its logic connotes that the output is a low signal “0” when and only when input 1 is TRUE and input 2 is FALSE. In other words, the result is “FALSE” when and only when the quenching agent input is “TRUE”. For other combinations of inputs: (a) both inputs are FALSE, i.e., (0, 0); (b) both inputs of quenched and recovered fluorescence are TRUE, i.e., (1,1); (c) only the input of recovered fluorescence is TRUE, i.e., (0, 1); the output of all the above inputs is “1” [34]. In the experiments of this chapter, an IMPLICATION logic gate for Fe3+ and AA fluorescence sensing was constructed based on the quenching effect of Fe3+ on the fluorescence intensity of Ag2S QDs and the oxidation of Fe3+ and the reduction of Fe3+ by AA. As shown in Fig. 5(a), Fe3+ and AA were used as inputs 1 and 2, respectively, and the fluorescence emission intensity of the prepared silver sulfide quantum dots was used as the output signal. As shown in Fig. 5(b), here we use the fluorescence intensity 450 as the fluorescence threshold. In the initial state, i.e., the input is (0, 0), the prepared GSH-Ag2S QDs emit a very strong fluorescence signal at 437 nm (ultra-pure water was added to keep the volume of the solution used to construct the logic gate consistent, so the fluorescence intensity is slightly decreased compared to that of the GSH-Ag2S QDs themselves), and the output of this high fluorescence signal is noted as “1 “. After the addition of Fe3+ to the Ag2S QDs system, i.e. (1, 0), the fluorescence intensity was significantly quench and the output was recorded as “0″. After adding AA to the Ag2S QDs system, i.e. (0, 1), the fluorescence intensity is basically unchanged and the output is recorded as “1″. After adding both Fe3+ and AA to the Ag2S QDs system, i.e. (1, 1), the fluorescence of the Ag2S QDs quench by Fe3+ can be restored because of the reducing property of AA, and the output is recorded as “1″. Therefore, by controlling different combinations of inputs of Fe3+ and AA, the operation logic of the IMPLICATION gate can be realized, and this logic platform can be used for simultaneous sensing of Fe3+ and AA.

In this work, silver sulfide quantum dots with high fluorescence emission intensity Ag2S QDs were successfully prepared efficiently and rapidly by a simple one-step microwave method. Using GSH as the sulfur source and AgNO3 as the silver source, compared with the existing synthesis process of Ag2S QDs, GSH is inexpensive, the microwave method is economical and the reaction time is short, so the work in this chapter also provides a new method for the preparation of low cost, short cycle time and low toxicity Ag2S QDs provides a new method. The selectivity of Ag2S QDs was analyzed, and the results showed that only Fe3+ could specifically burst the fluorescence of quantum dots among the 15 analytes. When the concentration of Fe3+ was varied within 400 μM ∼ 10 mM, its concentration showed a negative correlation with the fluorescence intensity of Ag2S QDs, and the lowest detection limit LOD for Fe3+ was 0.15 mM. In addition, the reduction of Fe3+ by AA was combined with the construction of the IMPLICATION logic platform by controlling different combinations of inputs. In summary, the Ag2S QDs prepared in this chapter can be used as a fluorescent nanoprobe and logic sensing platform to achieve selective detection of Fe3+, providing a simple new method for detecting Fe3+ in solution, which also has potential for development in the field of bio-computing.

Lijun Liu: Data curation, Formal analysis, Methodology, Resources, Writing – original draft. Lu Ga: Funding acquisition, Resources, Validation. Jun Ai: Funding acquisition, Visualization, Writing – review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The manuscript has not been published before and is not being considered for publication elsewhere. All authors have contributed to the creation of this manuscript for important intellectual content and read and approved the final manuscript. We declare there is no conflict of interest.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (Grant No. 21864020, 82160652), the Natural Science Foundation of Inner Mongolia (Grant No. and 2019MS02014, 2018MS02012), “Young Science and Technology Talents Program” (Leading Person) in Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region Colleges and Universities (Grant No. NJYT-19-A04), the Fundamental Research Funds for the Inner Mongolia Normal University, China, (Grant No. 2022JBZD013).
